title: Completion Flow (animation + queue drain, no reward effects)
deliverable: Tapping the circular checkbox marks a task complete: the card animates out (translateX+opacity+scale, 500ms), the next task slides in from below, and the completed task is appended to an in-memory `done` array. State is never mutated until the exit animation finishes. Queue drains to empty. No sound, no particles, no SVG checkmark draw, no glow ring.

in: Circular checkbox tap triggers completion sequence
Tap target ≥44px: 28px visual circle + 8px padding on all sides
Debounce / lock-out during animation window to prevent double-tap
Exit animation on completing card: translateX(80px) + opacity(0) + scale(0.95), ~500ms cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)
State update (remove from queue, push to done) fires AFTER animation completes (~500ms delay from tap)
Entrance animation on next task card: slides in from below (translateY(40px)→0 + opacity 0→1), ~350ms, begins after exit animation ends
In-memory `done` array: each entry is { id, text, completedAt }
Queue drains gracefully to zero items (empty state rendered by Block 11, but the drain itself is handled here)
When queue has multiple visible cards (show_count>1 from Block 3), only the top card is completable; others re-stack after removal
useQueue hook extended with a `completeTask(id)` action
Completing-card receives `data-completing='true'` attribute (or equivalent) so CSS/inline styles can apply the exit transform
Animation timing constants exported from a single location for reuse in Blocks 5–7
out: Persistence of done array to localStorage (Block 14)

files.purpose: Add completeTask(id) action: sets per-id animating flag, schedules state update after COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS, pushes to done array, removes from queue.
Accept `isCompleting` prop; apply exit inline-style transform when true. Render checkbox button with correct tap-target sizing and an onClick that calls onComplete(id).
Pass isCompleting and onComplete down to TaskCard. Render entrance animation on newly-promoted top card after previous card exits.
Single source of truth for timing constants: COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS (500), CARD_ENTER_ANIMATION_MS (350), EXIT_EASING, ENTER_EASING. Imported by hooks, components, and future reward blocks.
Manages in-memory done array (React state only, no localStorage). Exposes: done[], addDone(item), clearDone(). Consumed by useQueue and, later, Block 10 (done log) and Block 8 (fire streak).

implementation_steps.details: Export: COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS = 500, CARD_ENTER_ANIMATION_MS = 350, EXIT_EASING = 'cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)', ENTER_EASING = 'cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)'. These values are the single source of truth consumed everywhere animations run. Blocks 5, 6, 7 will import from here.

useState([]) for done array. addDone({ id, text, completedAt: Date.now() }) appends to array. clearDone() resets to []. Export { done, addDone, clearDone }. No localStorage, no side effects. This hook is lifted to the top-level provider or App.jsx so Block 8 (fire streak) and Block 10 (done log) can share the same instance.

implementation_steps.description: Extend useQueue.js: add completingIds Set and completeTask(id)
implementation_steps.details: Add a useState<Set<string>> named completingIds (tracks which task IDs are mid-animation). completeTask(id): (1) Guard — if completingIds.has(id) return immediately (double-tap prevention). (2) Add id to completingIds (triggers re-render → TaskCard receives isCompleting=true → CSS exit animation plays). (3) After COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS (via setTimeout), execute the state update: call addDone({id, text, completedAt}), call setQueue(q => q.filter(t => t.id !== id)), remove id from completingIds. The 500ms delay is the reward window — the user sees the card animate before it disappears.

implementation_steps.description: Update TaskCard.jsx: checkbox tap target and isCompleting exit animation
implementation_steps.details: Checkbox button: inline style width/height 44px, display flex, alignItems center, justifyContent center, background transparent, border none, cursor pointer, padding 8px (so visual circle is 28px, total hit area 44px). The 28px circle is a div with borderRadius 50%, border 2px solid gold/emerald color, inside the button. Pass isCompleting boolean prop. When isCompleting===true, apply inline transition style: { transform: 'translateX(80px) scale(0.95)', opacity: 0, transition: `transform ${COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS}ms ${EXIT_EASING}, opacity ${COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS}ms ${EXIT_EASING}` }. When false: { transform: 'none', opacity: 1 }. Do NOT use CSS classes — use inline styles per stack constraint.

implementation_steps.description: Card entrance animation for newly promoted task
implementation_steps.details: When queue[0] changes (previous top was removed), the new top card must animate in from below. Strategy: give each TaskCard a key={task.id}. When a new task.id appears at position 0, it is a fresh mount — apply an initial inline style translateY(40px)+opacity(0) via useState(true) inside TaskCard, then immediately transition to translateY(0)+opacity(1) over CARD_ENTER_ANIMATION_MS. Use a useEffect with requestAnimationFrame to flip the entering flag after mount so the transition fires: mount with entering styles → rAF → set entering=false → React re-renders with final styles → CSS transition plays. This avoids the need for a CSS class.

When show_count > 1 (from Block 3), secondary cards at indices 1, 2, 4 are rendered smaller/dimmer. Only queue[0] is the active task; its checkbox is fully interactive. Checkbox on secondary cards must be rendered but visually subdued and pointer-events: none (or disabled attribute) to prevent completing a non-top task. After queue[0] exits, queue[1] becomes the new queue[0] and receives the entrance animation (step 4.5). Secondary slots are refilled by the new queue ordering.

implementation_steps.description: Wire completeTask into Queue.jsx and verify timing sequence end-to-end
implementation_steps.details: Queue.jsx passes onComplete={completeTask} and isCompleting={completingIds.has(task.id)} to each TaskCard. Manual test sequence: (a) tap checkbox at t=0, (b) confirm card begins exit animation immediately, (c) confirm queue array is NOT yet updated at t=100ms (console.log or React DevTools), (d) confirm queue array is updated at t≥500ms, (e) confirm done array has the entry, (f) confirm new top card slides in from below. Also verify: rapid double-tap on same card fires completeTask once only.

Checkbox button must be focusable (no tabIndex=-1). onKeyDown: Space and Enter also trigger completeTask. aria-label='Görevi tamamla' on the button. role='checkbox', aria-checked='false' when pending, aria-checked='true' when isCompleting (the animation window) or after removal. Since the card unmounts after 500ms, aria-checked='true' is transitional.

data_structures.name: DoneItem
data_structures.shape: { id: string, text: string, completedAt: number }
data_structures.notes: id mirrors the queue item's id (uuid string from Block 2). completedAt is Date.now() (Unix ms). In-memory only for this block; Block 14 adds localStorage persistence, Block 18 adds Supabase write.

data_structures.name: completingIds
data_structures.notes: React state held inside useQueue. Contains IDs of tasks currently mid-exit-animation. Acts as a mutex: completeTask(id) is a no-op if id is already in the set. Cleared for each id after the setTimeout fires. Should never hold more than show_count entries simultaneously (in practice 1, since only the top card is interactive).

data_structures.name: QueueItem (unchanged from Block 2)
data_structures.notes: No new fields added. isCompleting is derived at render time from completingIds.has(item.id), not stored on the item itself.

interfaces.name: completeTask
interfaces.purpose: Initiates the completion sequence for the task with the given id. Guards against double-calls. Schedules state update after COMPLETION_ANIMATION_MS. Exported from useQueue.

interfaces.name: addDone
interfaces.purpose: Appends a completed task to the in-memory done array. Exported from useDone.

interfaces.name: clearDone
interfaces.purpose: Resets done array to []. Called by Block 10's Temizle button.

interfaces.name: TaskCard props
interfaces.signature: { task: QueueItem, isCompleting: boolean, onComplete: (id: string) => void, index: number, isInteractive: boolean }
interfaces.purpose: isCompleting drives exit animation. isInteractive controls whether the checkbox is pointer-events active (false for secondary cards when show_count>1). index used for progressive dimming (existing Block 3 behaviour).

acceptance_criteria: Tapping the checkbox on the top card begins the exit animation within one frame (≤16ms) — the card visually starts moving immediately on tap
The queue state (queue.length) does not decrease until 500ms ±50ms after tap — verifiable via React DevTools or console.log in a useEffect
The completed card reaches translateX(80px), opacity(0), scale(0.95) by 500ms
done[] has exactly one new entry with correct id, text, and completedAt timestamp within 510ms of tap
The new top card (if any) begins its entrance animation immediately after the exit animation ends (no gap > 1 frame)
New top card entrance: starts at translateY(40px)+opacity(0) and arrives at translateY(0)+opacity(1) within CARD_ENTER_ANIMATION_MS (350ms)
Double-tapping the checkbox rapidly fires completeTask only once — the second tap is ignored while the first animation is running
Checkbox tap target measures ≥44×44px in browser devtools (computed size)
Completing the last task in the queue results in an empty queue with done.length increased by 1; the Queue component renders empty state slot (handled by Block 11)
With show_count=3, completing the top card causes card-1 to become card-0 with entrance animation, and a new card-2 is revealed (or slot is empty if queue short)
Secondary cards (index>0) cannot be tapped to complete — pointer-events disabled on their checkboxes
Keyboard: Space and Enter on focused checkbox trigger completion identically to tap
verification: Manual: Open app with 3 tasks queued. Tap top checkbox. Observe card slides right and fades. Note time. Observe next card slides up. Open React DevTools → confirm queue.length decremented only after ~500ms.
Manual: Tap checkbox rapidly 3 times in quick succession. Confirm only one completion registers (done.length === 1, queue.length === 2).
Manual: Complete all tasks one by one. Confirm done.length equals original queue.length. Confirm queue is empty.
Manual: Set show_count to 3. Complete top task. Confirm second task becomes top with entrance animation. Confirm third slot attempts to show queue[2].
DevTools: Inspect checkbox button. Computed width and height both ≥44px.
DevTools: Inspect card during animation window (t=100ms): confirm transform includes translateX and opacity is >0 (animation still running). At t=600ms: card should be gone from DOM.
Keyboard: Tab to checkbox, press Space. Confirm completion animation fires.
Timing regression test: log Date.now() at tap and at state update. Delta must be 490–550ms.

questionable_decisions.assumption: CSS transition duration and setTimeout will stay roughly in sync. JS event loop lag is typically <16ms on idle main thread, so the card will have fully exited before state updates.
alternatives: Use the `transitionend` DOM event on the card element to trigger state update — guaranteed to fire exactly when animation is done, not 500ms ± jitter
Use a CSS animation with an animationend listener instead of inline transition
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Prefer `transitionend` event listener over setTimeout. Attach via ref in useEffect; clean up on unmount. This is resilient to frame drops, reduced-motion overrides, and browser throttling. setTimeout is simpler but can desync if the tab is backgrounded or the device is slow. However, if Block 6 (glow ring) needs to coordinate timing, a shared Promise resolved on transitionend is the cleanest cross-block contract.

questionable_decisions.decision: completingIds as a Set in React state (not a ref)
questionable_decisions.assumption: Putting completingIds in useState causes a re-render when an id is added (correct — TaskCard needs to receive isCompleting=true). A useRef would not trigger re-render.
alternatives: Use a useRef<Set> for completingIds and manually call forceUpdate — avoids a second render but is non-idiomatic
Store isCompleting directly on QueueItem objects — makes the shape mutable and harder to reason about
questionable_decisions.recommendation: useState(new Set()) is correct. Be aware that updating a Set in React state requires spreading into a new Set: setCompletingIds(prev => new Set(prev).add(id)). Using the same Set reference will not trigger a re-render.

questionable_decisions.decision: Entrance animation via useState inside TaskCard on mount (rAF flip pattern)
questionable_decisions.assumption: React will mount the new card with the initial 'entering' styles, and a requestAnimationFrame callback will fire before the next paint, allowing the transition to play.
alternatives: Use a CSS @keyframes animation on card mount — requires adding a className, which conflicts with the inline-styles-only stack constraint
Use the react-spring or framer-motion library for mount/unmount animations — adds a dependency, out of scope for this stack
Use a short setTimeout(0) instead of rAF — less precise but works in practice
questionable_decisions.recommendation: The rAF-flip pattern (mount with transform → rAF → clear transform) is correct for inline-styles-only stacks. Use a double rAF (rAF inside rAF) to guarantee the browser has committed the initial paint before starting the transition. Single rAF can occasionally be batched with the mount, causing the transition to be skipped.

questionable_decisions.decision: Only queue[0] is interactive (top-card-only completion model)
questionable_decisions.assumption: The PRD shows a single circular checkbox per card and emphasises one physical action at a time. Completing a non-top task would skip steps, violating the sequential queue contract.
alternatives: Allow any visible card to be completed (out-of-order), removing it from its position — more flexible but breaks chain ordering
Show checkboxes on all cards but animate a 'reorder' step first that promotes the card to the top before completing
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Enforce top-card-only completion as specified. Disable pointer-events on secondary checkboxes. If user research later shows desire for out-of-order completion, add as a deliberate opt-in, not the default.

questionable_decisions.decision: done array held in useDone hook lifted to App.jsx, not inside useQueue
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 8 (fire streak) and Block 10 (done log) both need to read done[] without importing useQueue. Separating concerns into useDone avoids a fat useQueue hook and prevents Blocks 8/10 from depending on Block 4's internal queue state.
alternatives: Keep done[] inside useQueue and have Block 8/10 call useQueue directly — simpler initially but couples unrelated blocks
Use React Context to share done[] — adds indirection, not needed at this scale
questionable_decisions.recommendation: useDone as a separate hook lifted to App.jsx is the right separation. useQueue receives addDone as a parameter (passed in from App.jsx). This keeps each hook focused and makes Block 14 (localStorage persistence) easy to add to useDone without touching useQueue.

open_questions: Should `reduced-motion` media query be respected? If so, the 500ms exit animation collapses to ~0ms and transitionend fires instantly — the state update must still be deferred by one tick to preserve the 'reward is felt' principle. Decision needed before Block 4 ship.

What happens if the user adds a new task (Block 9) while a completion animation is in flight? The new task appends to queue — does it affect the in-flight animation? Current spec assumes no interference since we only mutate after the timeout, but concurrent state updates should be tested.

The entrance animation starts 'after exit animation ends'. With transitionend approach this is natural. With setTimeout approach: should entrance start at t=500ms (same tick as state update) or t=500ms+1rAF? Exact sequencing should be locked before implementation.
When show_count > 1 and the queue has only 1 item left, completing it empties the queue. Should the empty-state card (Block 11) also animate in, or appear instantly? Coordinate with Block 11 author.

DoneItem.id: should it mirror the QueueItem.id exactly, or generate a new uuid at completion time? Mirroring is simpler; a new uuid is needed if the same task text could be re-added and re-completed (which is possible with chains). Current spec mirrors the id — confirm this is acceptable for Block 14/18 deduplication logic.
